name: Ports of La Vall d'Albaida Loop from Xàtiva
name_original: Los puertos carreteros de La Vall d'Albaida — Circular desde Xàtiva
description: This difficult 47.8-mile road cycling route from Xàtiva explores the scenic Vall d'Albaida, featuring mountain passes and the Bellús Reservo
region_intro: Road cycling around L'Alcúdia De Crespins offers diverse terrain, from riverine plains along the Sants River to the gentle hills of the Serra d'Enguera mountains. The municipality is characterized by varied topography, including terraced areas and leafy natural environments. This landscape provides a mix of flat sections and undulating routes, suitable for different levels of road cycling. The region's natural features contribute to scenic routes for exploration. Best road cycling routes around L'Alcúdia De Crespins The most popular road cycling route is Ports of La Vall d'Albaida Loop from Xàtiva, a 47.7 miles (76.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route explores the challenging ascents and scenic valleys of the La Vall d'Albaida region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Albufera de Anna – Port de Bosquet loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins, a difficult 56.9 miles (91.6 km) path. This route offers varied terrain, passing through natural areas and featuring significant climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Playa de Gandía – Tavernes Beach loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins, a 74.7 miles (120.2 km) trail leading through coastal plains and rural landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Road cycling around L'Alcúdia De Crespins is defined by riverine plains, gentle hills, and leafy nature areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult ascents. The routes in L'Alcúdia De Crespins are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 1350 road cyclists have used komoot to explore L'Alcúdia De Crespins's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: The region offers a diverse mix of terrain, from flat riverine plains along the Sants River to the gentle, undulating hills of the Serra d'Enguera mountains. This varied topography provides options for both leisurely rides and more challenging ascents, all set within beautiful, leafy natural areas.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in L'Alcúdia De Crespins for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858859653/ports-of-la-vall-d-albaida-loop-from-xativa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ports of La Vall d'Albaida Loop from Xàtiva&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 76.9 km route with over 1180 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674677" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna – Port de Bosquet loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 91.6 km with significant climbs.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling near L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: Many routes pass through scenic natural areas. You can explore the rich botanical environment around the Sants River, or incorporate visits to nearby natural monuments. Highlights include the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4586632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vikings' Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorgo de la Escalera&lt;/a&gt; with its clear pools, and the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4070836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera de Anna&lt;/a&gt; lake. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salto de Chella Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views.

Q: Are there historical landmarks accessible by road bike in the L'Alcúdia De Crespins area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. A notable landmark within cycling distance is the magnificent Xàtiva Castle, a double fortress with significant historical importance. You can also explore routes that pass by the 18th-century San Onofre Anacoreta Parish Church and the El Santísimo Cristo del Monte Calvario Chapel within L'Alcúdia de Crespins itself. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32070371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xàtiva Cathedral – Ateneo Sueco del Socorro loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins&lt;/a&gt; for a route that includes cultural sights.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: The Valencian Community generally offers excellent cycling weather for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with mild temperatures ideal for longer rides. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round cycling, though some cooler days may occur.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options around L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: Yes, the area provides several moderate and easy routes suitable for families or those seeking a less strenuous ride. The presence of riverine plains and gentle hills means you can find paths that are not overly challenging. There are 6 easy and 17 moderate routes available, offering plenty of choices for a relaxed family outing.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38213240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recreation Area – Xàtiva Cathedral loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 44.7 km circular route, perfect for exploring the local scenery and historical sites.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in L'Alcúdia De Crespins, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 14 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which offers a good mix of flat sections and undulating climbs, as well as the beautiful, leafy nature areas that make for scenic rides.

Q: Are there any less crowded road cycling routes in the L'Alcúdia De Crespins area?
A: L'Alcúdia De Crespins is described as a 'small and peaceful place' and a 'rural destination,' suggesting that many of its roads are quieter with less traffic compared to more urban areas. This provides an excellent opportunity to discover less crowded routes, especially those that venture into the surrounding natural landscapes and foothills of the Serra d'Enguera.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes available around L'Alcúdia De Crespins, catering to various skill levels. This includes 6 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 10 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every road cyclist.

Q: What are the surface conditions like on the road cycling trails?
A: The road cycling routes primarily utilize paved roads, offering smooth surfaces suitable for road bikes. While the region is known for its natural beauty, the focus for road cycling is on well-maintained asphalt, ensuring an enjoyable ride through the diverse landscapes of riverine plains and gentle hills.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to coastal areas from L'Alcúdia De Crespins?
A: Yes, you can embark on longer rides that extend towards the coast. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38242452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Gandía – Tavernes Beach loop from l'Alcúdia de Crespins&lt;/a&gt; is a 120.2 km trail that takes you through coastal plains and rural landscapes, offering a change of scenery from the inland routes.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as a difficult road ride, requiring very good fitness. It involves significant elevation gain and is best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging experience.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces, making for a generally smooth ride. However, given its difficulty, some segments might be unpaved or more challenging. The route takes you through diverse landscapes, including high hills, vineyards, and olive groves.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route features several notable points. You'll pass through mountain passes like Puerto de l'Olleria and Port de Salem and Beniarrés. You'll also see the scenic Bellús Reservoir and spillway. Beyond these, expect captivating landscapes with castles, charming towns like Bocairent, and views of natural enclaves.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: While the exact duration depends on your pace and stops, this difficult route, covering nearly 77 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, can take over 5 hours of riding for many cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a 'hard road ride' and requires 'very good fitness.' It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children due to its length, elevation gain, and challenging nature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The Vall d'Albaida region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are typically ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route from Xàtiva?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting from Xàtiva, you'll want to find suitable parking within the town. Xàtiva has various public parking options, including street parking and designated car parks, from which you can easily access the route's starting point.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while there aren't specific restrictions on dogs, it's generally not suitable for riding with pets. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier, and always adhere to local leash laws and regulations in towns or specific areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths in the region.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of 'La Defensa del Sur' (16% of the route), 'l’Olleria - Bélgida' (13%), and 'El Camí IT5TR5 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana' (10%), among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns and villages in the Vall d'Albaida region, offering opportunities to stop for refreshments. You can find cafes, restaurants, and shops in places like Bocairent or other charming towns along the way to refuel.
